name: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-3
title: Human-approval bypass must be a hard file marker, not conversation memory
description:
When the user grants or revokes the --no-human-approval bypass for rabbit-feature-touch Step 4, the state lives only in the conversation transcript. If the session resets, Claude drifts, or a new session starts, the authorization state is lost with no audit trail.

Observed: user said "I revoke the bypass human review permission" and Claude acknowledged verbally. No file was written. A future session would have no way to know the current grant/revoke state without re-reading the conversation.

Expected: the bypass permission should be a durable hard-file marker at repo root, analogous to .rabbit-scope-override. Presence of the marker = bypass granted; absence = Step 4 gate enforced. The marker should be gitignored.

Design:
- Marker file: .rabbit-human-approval-bypass (repo root, gitignored)
- Contents: "session" (mirrors .rabbit-scope-override semantics; no one-time variant needed since human-approval bypass is inherently session-scoped)
- rabbit-feature-touch dispatcher: at Step 4, check for .rabbit-human-approval-bypass before deciding to pass --no-human-approval. If marker present, bypass and emit a visible warning. If absent, wait for in-conversation approval as today.
- Grant path: user says "bypass human approval for this session" → dispatcher writes .rabbit-human-approval-bypass with content "session". User can also write it manually.
- Revoke path: user says "revoke bypass" → dispatcher deletes .rabbit-human-approval-bypass. Or user deletes it manually.
- sync-check.py: on every Stop, if .rabbit-human-approval-bypass exists, emit a red [rabbit] alert analogous to the scope-guard-off alert: "[rabbit] HUMAN APPROVAL BYPASS ACTIVE — Step 4 skipped for all rabbit-feature-touch dispatches".
- .gitignore: add .rabbit-human-approval-bypass.

This makes the grant/revoke auditable (file exists or doesn't), survives session resets, and produces a visible warning on every Stop so the user can't forget it's active.

history.note: Misrouted. Human-approval bypass is a workflow configuration setting owned by /rabbit-config, not tdd-subagent. Re-filed under rabbit-cage as RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-8 with /rabbit-config human-approval subcommand framing.
